error.msg: The scope of the variable 'temp' can be reduced.
error.verbose: The scope of the variable 'temp' can be reduced. Warning: Be careful when fixing this message, especially when there are inner loops. Here is an example where cppcheck will write that the scope for 'i' can be reduced:\012void f(int x)\012{\012 int i = 0;\012 if (x) {\012 // it's safe to move 'int i = 0;' here\012 for (int n = 0; n < 10; ++n) {\012 // it is possible but not safe to move 'int i = 0;' here\012 do_something(&i);\012 }\012 }\012}\012When you see this message it is always safe to reduce the variable scope 1 level.

error.msg: Assigning an integer to a pointer is not portable.
error.verbose: Assigning an integer (int/long/etc) to a pointer is not portable across different platforms and compilers. For example in 32-bit Windows and linux they are same width, but in 64-bit Windows and linux they are of different width. In worst case you end up assigning 64-bit integer to 32-bit pointer. The safe way is to store addresses only in pointer types (or typedefs like uintptr_t).

error.msg: Either the condition 'msg<=9-1' is redundant or the array 'TABLEAU_CHAINES[9]' is accessed at index 9, which is out of bounds.
error.verbose: Either the condition 'msg<=9-1' is redundant or the array 'TABLEAU_CHAINES[9]' is accessed at index 9, which is out of bounds.

location.info: Assuming that condition 'msg<=9-1' is not redundant

error.msg: Either the condition 'msg>=0' is redundant or the array 'TABLEAU_CHAINES[9]' is accessed at index -1, which is out of bounds.
error.verbose: Either the condition 'msg>=0' is redundant or the array 'TABLEAU_CHAINES[9]' is accessed at index -1, which is out of bounds.

location.info: Assuming that condition 'msg>=0' is not redundant

error.msg: The scope of the variable 'existe_tel' can be reduced.
error.verbose: The scope of the variable 'existe_tel' can be reduced. Warning: Be careful when fixing this message, especially when there are inner loops. Here is an example where cppcheck will write that the scope for 'i' can be reduced:\012void f(int x)\012{\012 int i = 0;\012 if (x) {\012 // it's safe to move 'int i = 0;' here\012 for (int n = 0; n < 10; ++n) {\012 // it is possible but not safe to move 'int i = 0;' here\012 do_something(&i);\012 }\012 }\012}\012When you see this message it is always safe to reduce the variable scope 1 level.
